title: Client onboarding checklist — what each user needs to connect to the Jarvis MCP
description:
Practical "what you need to start using this" reference for any user (including future tommaso on a new laptop, or pietro/daniel/etc).

## Prerequisites on the user's laptop

1. **Tailscale connected to the tailnet** (`tailscale status` shows nativedev as a peer).
   - This is the auth boundary: the MCP server is on Tailscale serve, NOT public Funnel. Only authenticated tailnet members reach it.
   - If new node: `tailscale up` and approve from admin.
2. **Claude Code** installed and logged in.
   - `claude login` once. The user's Max/Pro subscription pays for the model calls — server-side only proxies tools, never burns server's quota.
3. **uv** package manager (for `uvx mcp-proxy` bridge).
   - macOS: `brew install uv`
   - Linux: `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh`
   - Verify: `which uvx && uvx --version`
4. **(optional) Onshape browser tab** open on the document the user will mutate. This is just so the user sees regen live; the MCP doesn't require it.

## Configure the MCP

Two equivalent options:

A) Global (all projects):
```
claude mcp add --scope user onshape \
  --command uvx \
  --args "mcp-proxy https://nativedev.tail7d3518.ts.net:10001/sse"
```

B) Project-scoped (`.mcp.json` in repo root):
```json
{"mcpServers":{"onshape":{"command":"uvx","args":["mcp-proxy","https://nativedev.tail7d3518.ts.net:10001/sse"]}}}
```

Verify: `claude` → `/mcp` → "onshape" should be **connected** with ~60 tools.

## Per-user state on nativedev (server admin)

Each user needs:
- `~/.config/onshape-mcp/jarvis.env` (mode 600) with their `ONSHAPE_API_KEY`, `ONSHAPE_API_SECRET`, and a unique `MCP_PORT` (3000 = tommaso; pietro=3001; etc.).
- A systemd service instance: `sudo systemctl enable --now onshape-mcp@<username>`.
- A Tailscale serve mapping: `tailscale serve --bg --https=<10000+N> http://127.0.0.1:<3000+N>`.

The user-facing URL (`https://nativedev.tail7d3518.ts.net:10001/sse`) maps to ONE specific user. For multi-user, each user gets their own port: 10001 → tommaso (3000), 10002 → pietro (3001), etc.

## Smoke tests

- Network: `curl -s -m 5 https://nativedev.tail7d3518.ts.net:10001/sse | head -1` → `event: endpoint`
- MCP: `claude` → `> /mcp` → onshape connected
- End-to-end: in claude prompt, "use onshape MCP to list my documents and tell me how many you got"

## Failure modes to document

- **No tailnet**: SSE URL unreachable → `tailscale ping nativedev` first.
- **Claude not logged in**: subprocess hangs / 401. Run `claude login`.
- **uvx not found**: `mcp-proxy` command resolution fails. Install uv.
- **Onshape API keys rotated/invalid**: tools return 401. Server-side: update `jarvis.env`, restart `onshape-mcp@<user>`.

This bd issue should become the canonical onboarding doc — promote it into onshape-extension/CLIENT-SETUP.md when next-edited (current CLIENT-SETUP.md is close but lacks the multi-user port mapping table).

title: Phase 1: Deploy jarvis-onshape-mcp on nativedev with SSE + per-user systemd
description: Clone jarvis-onshape-mcp v1.2.0, install via uv, deploy as systemd template service onshape-mcp@.service in SSE mode (port 3000+ pool). Per-user API keys in ~/.config/onshape-mcp/<user>.env. Bootstrap tommaso from existing onshape-cadgen keys. Expose via Tailscale Funnel on port 10001.
acceptance_criteria: Service running for tommaso. curl https://nativedev.tail7d3518.ts.net:10001/sse returns SSE stream. /etc/onshape-mcp/users.toml created.

title: Phase 5: Real benchmark suite tests/benchmark_onshape_mcp.py
description: 5 prompts × N=5 runs comparing iframe-legacy vs Claude Code+Jarvis MCP. Wall-clock prompt→regen via Onshape currentmicroversion polling. Median ± p95 report in tests/reports/.

title: Phase 3: Skill consolidation — deprecate CadQuery skills, add onshape-design-assistant
description: Move cadquery-codegen, cadquery-validate, openscad-* to skills/_legacy/. Rewrite 3d-print-orchestrator → onshape-design-assistant SKILL.md. Update CLAUDE.md cardinal rules.

title: Phase 2: Client setup docs + mcp-remote bridge config
description: Document client-side mcp.json snippet using npx mcp-remote bridge. Verify /mcp lists ~60 onshape tools. Create CLIENT-SETUP.md.

title: Benchmark v2: bigger timeout, fresh doc per prompt family, persistent session for iterative
description:
Phase 5 first run (2026-04-30) showed:
- P1 simple cube: 60.78s median (1/2 success). Analytical estimate was 5-8s — 10x off.
- P2-P5: all timed out at 240s. Root cause suspected: doc state accumulation, no session persistence on iterative prompts (P4), retry loops on errors.

Next iteration of tests/benchmark_onshape_mcp.py should:
1. Default --timeout to 600s
2. Add --reset-doc flag that calls Onshape DELETE element + re-create Part Studio between prompt families
3. For P4 (iterative): reuse the P3 Claude session via `claude --resume <session_id>` instead of fresh subprocess
4. Write subprocess stdout/stderr to per-run logs in tests/reports/runs_<ts>/
5. Add A/B mode: temporarily re-enable onshape-cadgen and run same prompts through legacy path for direct comparison

Report at tests/reports/benchmark_20260430_071434.md has the full analysis.

title: Phase 4: Deprecate iframe extension (move backend+frontend to legacy/)
description: systemctl disable onshape-cadgen, move onshape-extension/{backend,frontend} → onshape-extension/legacy/. Add legacy/README.md explaining deprecation.
